daily_load_assessment: Your current TSB sits at +4.1 (CTL 29.7, ATL 25.6). This positive balance indicates you are fully recovered and physically prepared for high-intensity training. However, a CTL of under 30 is objectively low for someone aiming to improve competitive performance. Your body is ready for a high-stress stimulus today; do not waste this fresh state on junk miles.
acute_status_assessment_3_weeks: Over the last 21 days, you completed 14 sessions totaling 11.26 hours. While this frequency looks good on paper, 9 of these sessions were e-bike commutes. You have completed exactly zero performance cycling sessions in this block. While your running has kept a decent rhythm with 3 quality sessions, your cycling development has completely stalled due to a total lack of structured high-intensity work.
sport_trend_assessment_6_months: Your 180-day sport trend is highly stagnant. Your CTL has fluctuated in the high 20s to low 30s without any meaningful upward trajectory. This plateau is a direct consequence of relying on unstructured commuting to supply your training load. To drive adaptation for a faster 5km and to elevate your FTP above 250W, you must introduce structured progressive overload and stop counting commutes as primary training.
coach_verdict: Your body is fresh (TSB +4.1) and you have a massive deficit in high-intensity cycling performance over the last three weeks. To hit your goals of improving 5-minute and 20-minute cycling power, you must prioritize the Cycling Option today. No more excuses—it is time to put real power through the pedals.

cycling_option.workout_details: Warm-up: 15 mins progressive ramp to 180W, including 3x30-sec fast-cadence efforts. Main Set: 5x4 minutes @ 112-115% FTP (280W - 288W), with 4 minutes of very easy spinning recovery between intervals. Cool-down: 10 mins easy spinning at 130W.
cycling_option.reason: You have done zero performance cycling in the last 21 days. This workout directly targets your 5-minute peak power goal, stimulating cardiac output and VO2max adaptation while your freshness is high.

running_option.workout_details: Warm-up: 1.5 km easy jog, followed by dynamic stretches and 3x80m strides. Main Set: 4x1000m at your target 5km pace (aim for Zone 4/5 HR, around 175-182 bpm). Rest for 2 minutes (walking/slow jogging) between intervals. Cool-down: 1 km easy jog.
running_option.reason: To run a faster 5km, you must train at or slightly above target race pace. This session builds the mental and physiological tolerance required to maintain high pacing.

recovery_option.workout_details: Duration: 40 minutes flat road or trainer ride. Keep power strictly below 55% FTP (under 135W) and heart rate under 130 bpm. Cadence should remain smooth and fluid (85-95 rpm).
recovery_option.reason: If you are feeling any unrecorded musculoskeletal fatigue from recent run sessions, a strict recovery spin will promote blood flow and tissue repair without adding systemic stress.
